steps.title: Add and identify
steps.content: Therefore $$\sin(42\pi) = 0.$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice A is correct. The sine of a number $t$ is the y-coordinate of the point arrived at by traveling a distance of $t$ units counterclockwise around the unit circle from the starting point $\left(1,0\right)$. Since the unit circle has a circumference of $2π$ units, it follows that one full rotation around the circle is equal to a distance of $2π$ units. Therefore, a distance of $42π$ units around the circle from the starting point $\left(1,0\right)$ would result in exactly $21$ full rotations, arriving back at the point $\left(1,0\right)$. So, $\sin42π$ is equal to the y-coordinate of the point $\left(1,0\right)$, which is $0$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $\cos42π$, not $\sin42π$.
